mnemonics: D.O.T.S. for Struct Assignment: Direct (with .), Overall (at declaration), Typedef (with alias), Separate (member-by-member)
keyPoints: Use the dot operator (.) to assign values to individual members of a structure variable
You can initialize a structure at the time of declaration using curly braces {}
A structure's members can be assigned values separately after declaration, but must be done individually
commonMistakes: Trying to assign values to all members with a single assignment after declaration (e.g., student1 = {101, "Alice", 85.5};)
Forgetting that string assignments in character arrays require strcpy() instead of the assignment operator
Attempting to compare two entire structure variables directly instead of comparing individual members
